title: Add AI PM workspace regression tests
description: Add focused regression coverage for the macOS AI PM workspace data flow: state decoding, proposal actions, report deltas, safety policy, scheduling, and rollback support as those features land.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the core non-UI logic behind AI PM workspace behavior; tests run offline; new safety/scheduling/rollback code has direct coverage; macOS test target remains green.
close_reason: Regression coverage now includes state decoding and persistence, proposal action application, report deltas, safety policy enforcement, scheduler failure pausing, and rollback support; the macOS test target is green offline.

title: Add AI PM safety and autonomy controls
description: Define and enforce what the AI PM may do autonomously versus what requires explicit user approval. Include mutation budgets, risk thresholds, provider privacy boundaries, and confirmation gates for destructive or broad changes.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace exposes autonomy level and safety controls; high-risk or broad mutations require approval; server enforces limits independently of UI; prompts reflect the policy; tests cover enforcement for rejected autonomous actions.
close_reason: Added AI PM autonomy safety controls with action budgets, high-risk approval settings, server-side application rejection rules, prompt policy guidance, proposal apply approval gates, and regression coverage.

title: Add rollback for applied AI PM changes
description: Use AI PM audit metadata to support rollback of simple applied proposal actions so users can undo an accepted AI PM mutation from the macOS workspace.
acceptance_criteria: Applied proposal actions record enough before/after state for supported rollbacks; rollback UI is available from audit/proposal history; supported rollback cases are tested; unsupported rollback cases explain why they cannot be reversed automatically.
close_reason: Added rollback metadata for supported AI PM proposal actions, audit-row rollback controls, rollback audit events, status/field restore behavior, and regression coverage for rolling a status mutation back to its original column.

title: Harden AI PM autonomous scheduler
description: Make the macOS server's AI PM automation run reliably on configured cadence with clear pause/resume behavior, retry limits, failure reporting, and next-run visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled AI PM runs execute while the macOS server is running; manual disable/pause prevents runs; failures are persisted and visible; retry behavior is bounded; next-run timing updates correctly; tests cover schedule calculation and failure handling.
close_reason: Hardened AI PM scheduling with persisted consecutive failure counts, a configurable failure retry budget, automatic schedule pause after bounded failures, next-run visibility, UI controls, and regression coverage.

title: Complete macOS AI PM overview workspace
description: Finish the macOS AI PM overview as a first-class workspace with clear status, metrics, primary actions, pending decisions, project intelligence, recent reports, audit history, and LLM/server health.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting AI PM shows a polished workspace optimized for desktop scanning; primary actions are visible; empty/loading/error states are handled; no modal is required for routine AI PM monitoring; HIG review issues are addressed.
close_reason: Completed the macOS AI PM overview workspace with a desktop header, selected-board aware run action, provider/no-board availability states, dashboard metrics, status panel, empty states, and direct LLM settings access.

title: Build AI PM proposal review workflow
description: Make pending AI PM proposals easy to review, compare, apply, dismiss, or defer from the macOS workspace. The user should understand exactly what will change before accepting a proposal.
acceptance_criteria: Proposal rows expose risk/category/action count; selecting a proposal opens a detailed review surface; apply/dismiss/defer actions are explicit; invalid actions show actionable errors; applied proposal status updates and audit events are visible.
close_reason: Completed the proposal review workflow with a detailed review surface for every proposal, visible risk/category/action metadata, explicit accept/apply/defer/dismiss actions, safety policy messaging, and audit-preserving status updates.

title: Audit macOS AI PM current state
description: Review the current macOS AI PM branch against the intended product surface and write down what is complete, partial, missing, and risky. Turn findings into concrete acceptance criteria for the remaining beads.
acceptance_criteria: A concise current-state audit exists in the bead notes or project docs; missing work is mapped to child beads; any already-completed slices are identified so we do not duplicate work; next ready implementation bead is clear.
close_reason: Audited the current macOS AI PM implementation and documented complete, partial, risky, and missing work in docs/MACOS_AI_PM_AUDIT.md, mapped to the remaining macOS AI PM beads with a recommended execution order and done line.

title: Complete macOS AI PM product surface
description: Bring the macOS AI PM from a useful dashboard into a complete first-class project-management product surface. This includes the workspace UI, proposal review/apply workflow, autonomous scheduling, reports, rollback, notifications, settings, observability, and verification.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads for macOS AI PM workflow, autonomy, reporting, safety, rollback, and verification are either closed or explicitly deferred with rationale; the macOS app exposes the AI PM as a coherent first-class workspace rather than a collection of modal tools.
close_reason: Completed the macOS AI PM product surface: workspace UI, proposal review/apply/defer/dismiss, safety/autonomy enforcement, scheduler hardening, report history/export, rollback, notifications, provider observability, empty states, tests, and docs. Screenshot verification was explicitly deferred with rationale and no screenshots were committed per user request.

title: Create AI PM evaluation harness
description: Add repeatable fixtures and tests that exercise AI PM planning behavior against known board states. The harness should validate deterministic signal extraction, JSON response decoding, proposal application, and failure handling without calling a real LLM.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover project intelligence signals, proposal action application, invalid action handling, and AI PM state persistence; test fixtures represent healthy, blocked, stale, and complex parent-child boards; quality gates can run without network access or external models.
close_reason: Added a buildable macOS XCTest evaluation harness with healthy and complex fixtures covering project intelligence, JSON response decoding, proposal application, invalid action handling, and AI PM state persistence without LLM calls.

title: Build AI PM project intelligence signals
description: Derive deterministic project management signals from board state before calling the LLM: stale work, blocked work, missing owners, oversized beads, dependency cycles or orphaned children, stale in-progress items, and high-risk backlog clusters. Feed these signals into prompts and show them in the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Server computes a project intelligence summary without requiring an LLM; AI PM prompts include the summary; Mac and mobile dashboards show the latest signals; empty or healthy boards show a clear no-issues state.
close_reason: Added deterministic AI PM project intelligence signals, persisted latest signal state, fed signals into AI PM prompts, and exposed the latest signals on Mac and mobile dashboards.

title: Add AI PM audit history and rollback metadata
description: Record every AI PM run, proposal status change, and applied mutation in an auditable history. Include enough before and after metadata for users to understand what changed and for a later rollback feature to reverse simple accepted changes.
acceptance_criteria: AI PM dashboard exposes a history of runs and decisions; proposal application records action-level audit entries; audit records include actor, timestamp, board, proposal, action type, and before/after summaries; data is persisted on the Mac server and visible to mobile clients as read-only state.
close_reason: Added persisted AI PM audit history for runs, failures, proposal status changes, and applied proposal actions, with dashboard visibility and Go DTO support.

title: Expand AI PM proposal action model
description: Add richer structured proposal actions so AI PM suggestions can mutate project data beyond generic notes. Include action types for creating beads, updating fields, linking parent/child relationships, setting blockers, and changing status or priority. The UI should present these actions clearly before application, and server-side application should validate targets before mutating state.
acceptance_criteria: AI PM proposals can carry multiple typed actions; Mac review UI explains each action; applying supported actions mutates the board through existing store APIs; unsupported or invalid actions fail safely without partial silent mutation.
close_reason: Expanded AI PM proposal changes with top-level bead creation, explicit status, blocked, and stale actions; added validation/application support, prompt schema coverage, and review UI summaries.

title: Show AI PM schedule and run failures
description: Expose AI PM next-run timing and last-run failure state so scheduled automation is observable instead of silently failing.
design: Persist last run errors in AI PM state. Compute and expose next scheduled run from server settings. Show both in Mac and mobile AI PM dashboards.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboards show next run for scheduled automation, last error if a run fails, and clear the error after a successful run; scheduled failures are recorded without crashing.
close_reason: Added next-run scheduling metadata and last-run error visibility across server state, Mac dashboard, mobile dashboard, and Go DTOs.

title: Show AI PM dashboard on iPhone and iPad
description: Expose server-side AI PM state on iOS and iPadOS so mobile clients can see pending decisions, recent reports, run status, and trigger a server PM run.
design: Keep the server canonical. Mobile clients fetch AI PM state through the paired Mac server and present a read-oriented dashboard reachable from the existing Server controls.
acceptance_criteria: iPhone and iPad users can open AI PM from the app, refresh state from the server, trigger a server PM run when paired, and see pending decisions/recent reports without Mac-only dependencies.
close_reason: Added paired-server AI PM state fetch/run support and an iPhone/iPad dashboard in Server settings.

title: Apply AI PM proposals to beads
description: Let users review and apply AI PM proposal changes so accepted proposals can update bead fields, create child beads, add dependencies, or set parents.
design: Keep board state mutation explicit and auditable. Reuse existing BeadPlanReviewChange shape from AI plan review, present a confirmation sheet, then apply selected changes through BoardStore.
acceptance_criteria: Users can open a proposal, review its proposed changes, apply selected changes to the current board, and see the proposal marked accepted; invalid target IDs are reported without crashing.
close_reason: Implemented AI PM proposal review and application flow with reusable BoardStore mutation results.

title: Add AI PM dashboard
description: Expose autonomous AI PM outputs in the app so users can see pending decisions, reports, and automation status without digging into individual beads.
design: Make it a server/PM sheet reachable from the main toolbar. Keep copy text concise and operational.
acceptance_criteria: App shows AI PM settings, run status, pending decisions, and recent reports; users can run the PM manually and change autonomy settings.
close_reason: Added macOS AI PM dashboard for settings, run status, pending decisions, reports, and manual runs.

title: Run autonomous AI PM grooming
description: Let the server run an AI PM pass over board state to generate reports and decision proposals without requiring the user to craft prompts.
design: Use existing server-side LLM plumbing and structured JSON. Low-risk analysis is autonomous; board mutations remain proposed changes.
acceptance_criteria: User can trigger an AI PM run; run produces backlog/milestone/risk proposals and status summaries; no project state mutates without review.
close_reason: Implemented autonomous AI PM runs through server LLM plumbing, scheduled cadence, and structured proposals/reports.

title: Add autonomous AI PM state and settings
description: Persist AI PM automation settings, generated proposals, reports, and run metadata so the server can operate beyond one-off prompts.
design: Keep server state canonical. Store reviewable proposals separately from board mutation so autonomous work is auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Server stores AI PM settings and run results; clients can fetch settings/proposals/reports; state survives app restart.
close_reason: Implemented persistent AI PM settings, run state, proposals, and reports with server/client access.

title: Review and improve project plans with AI
description: Add an AI review flow that evaluates a proposed bead, epic, or subtree for missing steps, unrealistic scope, unclear acceptance criteria, dependency mistakes, and sequencing risk.
design: This is the PM-style review loop: critique the plan, suggest decomposition, identify blockers, and make the work more executable for coding agents.
acceptance_criteria: Users can request a plan review from a bead or hierarchy subtree; AI returns actionable findings and proposed changes; user can apply selected modifications; dependency and child issue suggestions are reviewable before mutation.
close_reason: Implemented AI plan review for beads and subtrees with findings, proposed changes, and explicit apply actions.

title: Suggest complete bead fields from partial drafts
description: Let the AI project manager fill in missing bead form fields from a partial draft, existing project context, and selected parent or board context.
design: Return structured field suggestions with rationale. Do not overwrite user-entered fields unless the user explicitly accepts a replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Users can ask for AI suggestions while creating or editing a bead; suggestions include description, acceptance criteria, labels, priority, issue type, parent, and dependencies where useful; user can accept individual fields or reject the proposal.
close_reason: Implemented AI bead field suggestions across Swift clients, macOS server endpoint, and Go client API.

title: Add server-side LLM configuration and status
description: Add Mac-server-only configuration for local or remote LLM providers, including provider status, model selection, and safe failure behavior for clients.
design: Keep AI execution server-side. Clients should only know whether planning assistance is available and what actions can be requested.
acceptance_criteria: Mac server exposes LLM availability/status; configuration is not required on iOS or iPadOS; missing or failing LLM providers degrade gracefully; no API keys or local model paths are exposed to clients.
close_reason: Added Mac-server-only LLM provider configuration, sanitized availability status in health and /llm/status, client status display, and Go DTO/client support without exposing API keys.

title: Build AI project manager planning assistant
description: Add a server-side AI project manager capability that helps turn vague work into realistic beads, hierarchy, dependencies, risk notes, acceptance criteria, and progress reporting. The goal is not simple autocomplete; it should automate planning and reporting work that a project manager would normally perform.
design: AI PM should operate over canonical server state, repository context, and beads hierarchy. It should produce reviewable structured patches rather than silently mutating project state.
acceptance_criteria: Server owns AI configuration and execution; clients can request planning help; AI returns structured proposals for user review; proposals can create or update beads, parents, dependencies, and reporting summaries only after explicit user confirmation.
close_reason: Completed the AI PM workflow with field suggestions, plan review, structured apply actions, and board/subtree status reports.

title: Add parent and child issue controls
description: Add buttonology and forms for creating child beads, selecting or changing a parent bead, and navigating between parent and child beads from cards and detail views.
design: Children remain independent cards in Kanban columns. Parent cards show compact child links/counts, and create-child defaults the parent field to the current bead.
acceptance_criteria: Bead editor supports selecting a parent; detail view has create-child and parent/child navigation controls; card previews show child counts and clickable child links where space allows; changes sync through the server model.
close_reason: Added parent selection, create-child flow, relationship navigation controls, and Kanban child link previews.

title: Add hierarchy-focused beads view
description: Add a cross-platform view that shows beads as an outline/tree instead of a Kanban board, using parent/child relationships while preserving quick navigation back to the board and selected bead detail.
design: Kanban remains the workflow view. Hierarchy view is a separate structural view for epics, milestones, and child work.
acceptance_criteria: Users can switch to a hierarchy view; parent beads expand to show children; rows expose type, status, priority, and blocked state; selecting a row navigates to the bead detail; iPhone, iPad, and macOS layouts are usable.
close_reason: Added a cross-platform hierarchy workspace with expandable parent/child rows, relationship-aware metadata chips, row selection into bead detail, iPhone navigation, and board/hierarchy switching.

title: Model beads hierarchy and dependency relationships
description: Preserve native beads structure in the app model and server payloads so parent/child hierarchy, issue type, and dependency relationships can drive UI and later planning automation.
design: Treat hierarchy and dependencies as separate concepts. Parent/child is structural ownership. Dependencies are workflow/blocking edges. Keep every bead movable independently on the Kanban board.
acceptance_criteria: Imported beads preserve beads ID, issue type, parent ID, dependency IDs/counts, and dependent IDs/counts; server/client JSON includes the relationship fields; existing boards migrate without data loss.
close_reason: Preserved native beads hierarchy and dependency metadata across import, model persistence, server JSON, Swift clients, and Go DTOs.

title: Add compact iOS board switcher
description: The iPhone compact client needs an obvious way to switch between boards from the main board screen. It currently shows whichever board is selected internally, making additional server boards hard to discover.
close_reason: Added compact iPhone board switcher menu with selected-board title.

title: Fix iOS client board discovery from Mac server
description: iOS clients should pick up boards created on the canonical Mac server during refresh/sync. Currently new server-side boards do not appear reliably on iOS.
close_reason: Fixed iOS/iPadOS paired-server refresh and protected server-only boards during client saves.

title: Add optimized iPad landscape workspace
description: Create a dedicated regular-width landscape iPad layout that is meaningfully different from portrait and gives the board more horizontal working space.
acceptance_criteria: Landscape iPad uses board + inspector without the persistent sidebar; portrait iPad keeps split-view behavior; builds pass.
close_reason: Landscape iPad already uses a dedicated board-plus-inspector workspace without the persistent sidebar; portrait keeps split-view behavior and builds pass.

title: Fix compact iPhone board navigation
description: Replace squashed split board with a compact one-column board flow.
acceptance_criteria: iPhone shows one column at a time, native toolbar/search, and pushes bead detail instead of showing squeezed split panes.
close_reason: Implemented compact iPhone one-column board flow with inline search and native detail navigation.

title: Tune iPad and macOS layouts
description: Improve regular-width iPad and macOS density, inspector, toolbar, and adaptive columns.
acceptance_criteria: iPad split view preserves board/detail clarity; macOS detail is leading-aligned and toolbar/sidebar/card density feel native.
close_reason: Tuned iPad/macOS board sizing and added macOS inspector layout.

title: Adaptive shells for iPhone, iPad, and macOS
description: Implement Apple HIG-driven adaptive layouts while preserving shared code reuse.
acceptance_criteria: iPhone uses compact one-column workflow; iPad uses optimized split view; macOS uses denser desktop inspector.
close_reason: Adaptive platform UI pass completed and builds verified.

title: Verify macOS AI PM UX with screenshots
description: Run the macOS app and capture representative AI PM workspace screenshots across empty, healthy, pending-decision, report-history, and failure states. Use the screenshots to catch layout, readability, and HIG issues.
acceptance_criteria: Representative screenshots are captured or documented; layout issues are filed as beads or fixed; AI PM workspace is checked at small and large macOS window sizes; README screenshots are updated if appropriate.
notes: Screenshot capture remains local-only per user request and could not be completed in this automation pass because macOS launched the app process without exposing a capturable window after relaunch. Do not add data-bearing screenshots to the repo.

title: Improve AI PM LLM/provider observability
description: Expose provider health, model, latency, last failure, request limits, and endpoint configuration clearly inside the macOS AI PM workspace so AI PM failures are diagnosable without hunting through settings.
acceptance_criteria: AI PM workspace shows provider/model/status/latency/failure state; settings are reachable from the workspace; endpoint test/discovery results are visible; failure messages guide the user to next action; no API keys or secrets are displayed.
close_reason: Expanded the macOS AI PM status panel with provider, endpoint, model, latency, failure, timeout, response cap, retry limit, and direct settings access while avoiding API key exposure.

title: Add macOS AI PM empty and onboarding states
description: Make the macOS AI PM workspace useful when no provider, no board, no reports, no proposals, or no prior run exists. Guide users through the minimal setup path without fake sample tickets.
acceptance_criteria: Empty states are specific to missing board/provider/pairing/report/proposal conditions; primary setup actions are available; no sample AI PM content appears as real state; first-run path is clear and HIG-consistent.
close_reason: Covered macOS AI PM empty and onboarding states with no-board/provider availability banners, first-run status messaging, empty pending decisions/intelligence/reports/audit sections, direct LLM settings access, and no fake sample AI PM state.

title: Improve AI PM report history and exports
description: Turn AI PM reports into a usable project-manager history on macOS, with since-last-run deltas, section filters, copy/export affordances, and a useful detail view.
acceptance_criteria: Report history is browsable from the AI PM workspace; progress/risks/blockers/decisions are visually separated; users can copy or export a report; report detail handles long content cleanly; mobile-readable server state remains compatible.
close_reason: Improved AI PM report history with visible delta groups, long-section detail disclosure, and a Copy Markdown export affordance for reports in the macOS workspace.

title: Add AI PM notification routing to workspace
description: Make macOS AI PM notifications lead users back to the AI PM workspace context and clearly identify whether the alert is about a high-risk proposal, run failure, stale blocker, or required decision.
acceptance_criteria: Notification settings are visible in AI PM workspace; notification copy is specific; opening/responding to a notification brings users to the AI PM workspace when possible; run failures and high-risk decisions are covered; notification behavior is tested or manually verified.
close_reason: Added AI PM notification route metadata, macOS notification response handling that opens the AI PM workspace, foreground presentation behavior, and visible high-risk/run-failure notification toggles in the workspace.

title: Refactor AI PM workspace into dedicated components
description: Move the macOS AI PM workspace out of the large RootView file into focused SwiftUI components so the workspace, dashboard cards, proposal list, report list, and audit list can evolve without making RootView harder to maintain.
acceptance_criteria: RootView only wires workspace mode selection; AI PM workspace components live in dedicated files; sheet and workspace entry points reuse shared components; macOS and iOS builds still type-check.
close_reason: Moved macOS AI PM workspace, dashboard sheet, proposal review/apply views, report rows, project intelligence view, and audit rows out of RootView into dedicated AIPMWorkspaceViews.swift, wired the file into iOS and macOS targets, and verified builds/tests.

title: Polish mobile AI PM workspace
description: Make the iPhone and iPad AI PM workspace feel like a first-class remote planning surface instead of a form transplanted from Server settings. Surface paired-server status, pending decisions, high-risk decisions, project intelligence, reports, audit history, refresh, and run actions in a layout that works for compact iPhone and larger iPad views.
acceptance_criteria: Remote AI PM workspace has a scan-friendly layout with status metrics and primary actions; iPad gets a dashboard-style surface rather than only a settings form; compact iPhone remains readable and scrollable; remote dashboard sheet still works; iOS and macOS builds/tests pass.

title: Polish AI PM workspace layout
description: Make the AI PM workspace feel like a first-class planning surface rather than a modal form transplanted into the content area. Add an overview/action layout that surfaces run status, pending decisions, project intelligence, reports, and audit history for scanning on desktop and tablet.
acceptance_criteria: AI PM workspace has a workspace-appropriate layout with visible status metrics and primary actions; pending decisions, reports, and project intelligence are easy to scan without opening another sheet; existing sheet entry points still work; compact mobile remains usable; macOS tests/build pass.
close_reason: Reworked the macOS AI PM workspace into a scan-friendly dashboard with metric tiles, status, automation controls, pending decisions, project intelligence, recent reports, and audit history while preserving the sheet dashboard and mobile remote workspace behavior.

title: Add AI PM workspace view
description: Add AI PM as a first-class workspace view alongside Board and Hierarchy. The view should surface AI PM status, project intelligence, pending decisions, reports, audit history, and run/settings actions instead of hiding that work in a modal dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace mode includes an AI PM view on macOS, iPad, and iPhone where appropriate; selecting AI PM replaces the main content area rather than opening only a sheet; macOS can run/save AI PM controls from the view; mobile can refresh/run the remote AI PM and see pending decisions/reports; existing dashboard sheets can be reused or decomposed without duplicating logic; builds/tests pass.
close_reason: Added AI PM as a first-class workspace mode across macOS, iPad portrait, iPad landscape, and iPhone; refactored local and remote AI PM dashboards into reusable workspace content while preserving sheet entry points; macOS workspace can run/save AI PM settings and mobile workspace can refresh/run remote AI PM state.

title: Add hierarchy graph auto-arrange
description: Add a user-facing auto-arrange control to the hierarchy graph so dragged or messy ticket relationship layouts can be reorganized into a readable graph without manually moving every node.
close_reason: Added relationship-aware hierarchy graph auto-arrange with component grouping and centered source placement

title: Polish LLM endpoint discovery and hierarchy graph UX
description: Add endpoint base URL convenience so users can enter only a host/IP and the app discovers the correct OpenAI-compatible base path. Revisit the hierarchy view so the graph implementation is actually visible and suitable for relationship visualization/editing instead of a ticket list.
close_reason: Implemented LLM endpoint path discovery and interactive hierarchy graph editing

title: Improve LLM settings model discovery and testing
description: Update the LLM settings dialog so it can discover available models from a configured endpoint, allow endpoints without API keys, provide model selection instead of manual entry, and expose Apply and Test Endpoint actions.
close_reason: Added LLM endpoint model discovery, endpoint testing, optional API key handling, model picker support, Apply and Save & Close actions, and offline tests for model response decoding and unauthenticated endpoint status.

title: Turn hierarchy view into dependency graph
description: Replace the current list-like hierarchy view with a graph-oriented view that makes ticket relationships visible, including dependency and blocker edges.
close_reason: Implemented hierarchy graph view with parent and dependency edges.

title: Harden LLM provider operations
description: Improve LLM provider behavior for real-world use: model selection, request timeouts, retry and backoff, response size limits, privacy controls, cost and latency visibility, and clear provider diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: LLM settings include model and timeout controls; requests enforce timeout and size limits; transient provider failures retry with backoff; status shows last latency and failure; prompts avoid sending unnecessary server secrets or auth material.
close_reason: Added configurable provider timeout, response size cap, retry/backoff, optional API-key request handling, latency/failure diagnostics, macOS settings controls, Go DTO fields, and offline configuration tests.

title: Add AI PM notifications for decisions and risks
description: Notify users when AI PM automation produces high-risk proposals, blocked work, stale in-progress items, or reports requiring a decision. Use platform-appropriate local notifications and keep notification controls in the AI PM settings.
acceptance_criteria: Users can enable or disable AI PM notifications; high-risk new proposals and run failures can trigger local notifications on Mac; mobile clients surface unread decision state after refreshing from the server; notification copy points users to the AI PM dashboard.
close_reason: Added AI PM notification settings, macOS local notifications for high-risk proposals and run failures, mobile pending/high-risk decision indicators after refresh, Go DTO fields, and regression tests for legacy settings and attention state.

title: Add AI PM recurring reports and deltas
description: Make AI PM reporting useful as a recurring project manager summary. Compare the current board to the previous AI PM snapshot, identify progress, regressions, new risks, resolved blockers, and recommended next decisions.
acceptance_criteria: AI PM reports include since-last-run deltas; report UI separates progress, risks, blockers, and decisions; reports persist with timestamps; mobile clients can read report history from the server.
close_reason: Added persisted board snapshots to AI PM reports, deterministic since-last-run deltas for progress, risks, blockers, and decisions, prompt context from the previous report snapshot, mobile-readable DTO fields, and legacy report decode tests.

title: Generate AI project status reports
description: Add AI-generated project reporting over boards, epics, and hierarchy subtrees, summarizing progress, risks, blockers, stale work, and likely next actions.
design: Reporting should use canonical server state plus hierarchy/dependency structure. It should be terse, operational, and focused on decisions and next actions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can generate a status report for a board or parent bead; report distinguishes completed, active, blocked, stale, and unplanned work; output can be copied or saved as notes; no project state is changed without confirmation.
close_reason: Completed the AI PM workflow with field suggestions, plan review, structured apply actions, and board/subtree status reports.

title: Visualize selected parent relationships on the board
description: When a parent bead is selected, provide an optional board overlay that visually connects the parent card to visible child cards and indicates children hidden by scroll, filter, board, or column visibility.
design: Relationship lines are a temporary selection affordance, not always-on decoration. Avoid visual noise and preserve Kanban readability.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a parent can show unobtrusive relationship lines to visible children; hidden children are summarized; overlay does not interfere with drag/drop, scrolling, or compact iPhone layouts; users can disable or ignore the overlay.
close_reason: Added an optional board relationship overlay with parent/child highlighting, dashed connector lines, hidden-child summary, and compact iPhone excluded.

title: Add cross-platform Go client
description: Add a cross-platform Go command-line client in the same repository without wiring it into the Xcode project. It should build via Makefile and talk to the existing Beads-Orchestrator Mac server API.
close_reason: Added isolated Go CLI and Fyne UI clients with Makefile build support.

title: Document macOS AI PM workflow
description: Update project documentation to explain the macOS AI PM workflow, server-canonical model, autonomy settings, proposal review, reports, notifications, and limitations.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs describe how to configure and use macOS AI PM; autonomy/safety behavior is explicit; screenshots are included when available; limitations and provider setup are documented.
close_reason: Documented the macOS AI PM workflow in README, including provider setup, workspace flow, autonomy/safety policy, scheduling, rollback limitations, reports, observability, and notification routing. New screenshots were intentionally not added because the user asked not to push data-bearing screenshots.

title: Design and wire app icon into Xcode project
description: Create a Beads-Orchestrator app icon and add it directly to the Xcode project asset catalogs for macOS, iOS, and iPadOS.
close_reason: Generated and wired shared AppIcon asset catalog for macOS, iOS, and iPadOS.
